Name: dynamic_precision_routing
Title: Dynamic Precision Routing for Training-Free Adaptive Quantization
Short Hypothesis: Can a training-free dynamic precision routing framework optimize mixed-precision quantization for architecture-specific performance while maintaining theoretical guarantees on stability and efficiency?
Related Work: Existing works like EMQ and SNIP focus on training-free methods for quantization and dynamic precision adjustments but often lack a comprehensive framework that integrates deterministic spectral diagnostics and adaptive routing. This proposal distinguishes itself by incorporating a rigorous mathematical foundation for quantization stability and implementing a dynamic routing strategy that adapts to model architecture and data distributions without retraining.
Abstract: In the realm of machine learning, optimizing models for efficient inference remains a critical challenge, particularly with mixed-precision quantization techniques. Current state-of-the-art methods often rely on heuristic approaches or require extensive retraining, leading to inefficiencies. This proposal introduces a novel dynamic precision routing framework designed to operate in a training-free manner. By leveraging curvature-aware diagnostics and geometric analysis, our approach dynamically adjusts the precision of individual layers based on their sensitivity to quantization. We employ a robust theoretical foundation to ensure stability during the routing process, explicitly quantifying the trade-offs between computational efficiency and model performance. The proposed framework not only enhances the accuracy-complexity trade-off but also integrates seamlessly with existing architectures, making it a versatile tool for diverse applications. The effectiveness of our approach will be validated through rigorous empirical evaluations across various benchmarks.
Experiments: Evaluate the dynamic precision routing framework on standard datasets (e.g., ImageNet, CIFAR-10) using different model architectures (e.g., ResNet, ViT).
Conduct ablation studies to assess the impact of different routing strategies and precision levels on inference accuracy and computational overhead.
Compare performance metrics, including accuracy, latency, and energy consumption, against baseline mixed-precision quantization methods.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may exhibit unpredictable performance on highly variable datasets, necessitating further empirical validation.
Dynamic routing may introduce additional computational overhead during inference, which should be carefully measured against the performance gains.
The theoretical guarantees provided may not hold under all practical scenarios, requiring ongoing refinement and adaptation of the framework.
